fragility_points: A fast macro risk-off or crypto-specific selloff before resolution.
BTC drifting back toward the 72k line, making noon ET microstructure decisive.
Thin independent contextual catalyst evidence in this run due to blocked/low-yield web fetches.
key_assumptions: No major macro or crypto-specific downside shock arrives before noon ET on April 17.
Binance remains a stable and usable resolution venue.
A roughly 2.8% cushion above the strike is not erased by ordinary short-term volatility.
main_logical_chain: The contract settles on Binance BTC/USDT at a specific noon ET one-minute close, so current Binance price location matters directly.
BTC is currently several percent above 72k and recent trading has mostly held above the strike.
With only about two days left, the default path is continued trade above 72k unless a distinct downside catalyst arrives.
Because the settlement design is path-sensitive, residual No probability should remain meaningful rather than collapsing toward certainty.
main_thesis: BTC is currently trading with a meaningful cushion above 72k and, absent a fresh downside shock before noon ET April 17, is modestly more likely than the market implies to settle above the strike.
quote_anchors: Binance BTC/USDT 12:00 ET one-minute candle close higher than 72,000
BTC traded around 74040.86 during the run
source_quality_view: High quality on direct rules and price-source verification, but only moderate quality on broader near-term catalyst context because some contextual web sources were blocked or thin.
strongest_disconfirmers: The market resolves on a single one-minute noon ET candle close, so a brief downside move can still flip the result.
Recent Binance ranges show BTC can move several percent on this horizon.
strongest_supports: Binance BTCUSDT traded around 74040.86 during the run, about 2.83% above the strike.
Recent Binance daily closes held in the mid-74k area rather than near 72k.
No clearly identified scheduled catalyst before resolution looked strong enough to dominate the next ~51 hours.
timing_relevance: Timing is central because this is not a daily close market; one specific Binance one-minute close at 12:00 ET on April 17 decides the contract.
unresolved_ambiguities: Whether any unscheduled downside catalyst appears before noon ET April 17.
How much noon ET print-specific noise matters if BTC is only marginally above the strike by then.
what_would_change_view: I would cut the estimate if BTC re-entered the 72k-73k zone, if a clear downside macro/crypto catalyst emerged before resolution, or if Binance-specific operational issues appeared.
